summary: R1: GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 deterministically stops the #146 stdin-prompt hang — git refuses to prompt for credentials/host keys, exits non-zero fast, and the executor's existing fetch-fail -> local-HEAD+WARN fallback (mindspec_executor.go:116, covered by executor_test.go bad-URL test) fires. Env-only is SUFFICIENT for the observed (prompt) hangs; deferring the exec.CommandContext wall-clock timeout for the rarer non-prompting slow-network hang as a Non-Goal is acceptable and well-justified (every #146 hang was a stdin prompt; a timeout adds a tunable knob + new abort-legit-fetch failure surface). The execCommand seam is clean: the house idiom is cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0") which APPENDS (does not clobber inherited env) — already the pattern at gitops_test.go:94/115/145. Setting it uniformly on fetch/push/remote-show/symbolic-ref (per resolved Open Question) is harmless on the non-network symbolic-ref. NON-BLOCKING impl guidance: implementation must append, not assign a fresh slice; and swapExec (gitops_test.go:276) currently captures only name+args, so the env-assertion test needs a small extension to capture cmd.Env. R4: switching fetchChildren from the hardcoded open,in_progress,closed triple to bead.AllStatuses(root) exactly matches the status BREADTH of advanceState/queryAllChildren (complete.go:856 also iterates bead.AllStatuses(root) = builtins open/in_progress/blocked/closed + project customs read from .beads/config.yaml status.custom). AllStatuses needs root only to read the config file for customs; bd itself runs in cwd (no -C), and CustomStatuses degrades gracefully to nil on a missing/unreadable file, so even root=="" yields builtins (still adds blocked vs today). No perf/error concern: it is a cheap local file read, not a DB read, on the cache warm path. Including blocked children DOES change derivation — e.g. {2 closed, 1 blocked} flips review->implement in DerivePhaseFromChildren (blocked is counted into lifecycleTotal but is neither closed nor in_progress, so it reads as open-equivalent) — but that shift is the INTENDED fix: it converges the cache view with advanceState (the authority) and with ADR-0023 line 108 ('some closed, some open, some blocked -> plan; agent must unblock'). This aligns derivation, it does not introduce a new divergence. NON-BLOCKING design subtlety: the fix MUST keep fetchChildren a SINGLE comma-joined bd call (--status=<join(AllStatuses,",")>), NOT a per-status fan-out like queryAllChildren — a fan-out would break TestCache_GetChildren_MemoizesPerEpic (asserts 1 call/epic) and the GetChildren 'single bd call' contract; the spec only mandates status BREADTH equivalence, so single-call is the correct reading. fetchAllEpics carrying the same literal triple is a SOUND out-of-scope boundary: epics are not run through the child-status-count derivation R4 fixes (an epic's own status drives idle/closed; phase derives from its CHILDREN), and ADR-0023's blocked-counting rule is about children, not epics. Minor latent inconsistency (a blocked epic would be invisible to AllEpics) worth a future follow-up, but a distinct concern, not this bug. APPROVE: no env-clobber mandated, R4 matches advanceState, the behavioral shift is the documented intended fix.
